name: APOS Legal
legal_name: APOS Legal Rechtsanwaltsgesellschaft mbH & Co. KG
description: German litigation and dispute resolution for US and UK companies. Full-service representation in German courts by a German-admitted attorney (Rechtsanwalt) with 20+ years of experience and 5,000+ clients represented. All communication in English.

registration.management_company: APOS Legal Management- und Rechtsanwaltsgesellschaft mbH

attorney.name: Fatih Bektas
attorney.title: Rechtsanwalt & Fachanwalt für Arbeitsrecht
attorney.background: Former FinTech CEO and board member with 10 years in executive roles

target_clients: US companies with disputes in Germany
UK companies with disputes in Germany (post-Brexit)
International businesses needing German court representation
Foreign investors and shareholders in German companies
Businesses needing to enforce foreign judgments in Germany

key_facts.mandatory_representation: Attorney representation (Anwaltszwang) required at Landgericht and above (disputes over EUR 5,000)
key_facts.fee_system: Statutory fee schedules (RVG/GKG), not hourly billing
key_facts.cost_rule: Loser-pays: the losing party pays both sides' statutory costs
key_facts.discovery: No US/UK-style discovery — each party provides its own evidence
key_facts.statute_of_limitations: Standard 3 years (§ 195 BGB), calculated from year-end
key_facts.remote_handling: No personal appearance required — attorney handles everything remotely

tools.name: Litigation Cost Calculator
tools.description: Estimate German court fees and statutory attorney fees based on dispute value (EUR 5,000 to EUR 100,000,000). Includes RVG fee breakdown, GKG court fees, and three outcome scenarios (win, lose, settle).

tools.name: Deadline Checker
tools.description: Calculate response deadlines for German court documents (Verteidigungsanzeige, Mahnbescheid, Vollstreckungsbescheid, Versäumnisurteil, Berufung) and statute of limitations for multiple claim types.

tools.name: Dismissal Checker
tools.description: 3-minute assessment of German employment law dismissal risk. Evaluates company size, employment duration, dismissal reason, prior warnings, social selection (Sozialauswahl), and special protection status. Returns risk level with legal references (KSchG, BGB §622, §623).

Resolution through litigation, settlement, arbitration, or negotiation
